title: Internal Control Module Software Incompatibility
system: Internal Control Module Software Incompatibility
possible_causes: Internal Control Module Software Incompatibility fault.
Module software, calibration, configuration, replacement/programming mismatch, network configuration, variant coding, power interruption during programming, or related module DTC influence.
Calibration IDs, software compatibility, coding, initialization, and programming procedures are vehicle-specific and must be confirmed in official service information.
check_order: Save all-module DTCs, freeze-frame data, module part numbers, calibration IDs, coding/configuration data, programming history, battery voltage, and related communication DTCs.

Confirm vehicle-specific software compatibility, module variant coding, configuration, initialization, immobilizer/security steps, and programming prerequisites in the manufacturer service manual or OEM service system.
Inspect battery support, module power/ground, network communication, installed module part numbers, calibration levels, and configuration/coding consistency.
Separate calibration/configuration mismatch, wrong module, incomplete programming, network, power supply, and module fault causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating software incompatibility as a parts fault before checking coding and calibration.
Programming without battery support, OEM prerequisites, or recovery plan.
Assuming module part numbers, calibration levels, or security initialization are interchangeable across vehicles.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around programming/coding, immobilizer/security, SRS, brake, steering, high-voltage, and ADAS modules; failed programming or incorrect coding can immobilize the vehicle or disable safety systems, so follow OEM procedures and recovery steps.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance
